title: Backend Server Error Code Distribution (F5 BIG-IP)
spl:
index=network sourcetype="f5:bigip:ltm:http"
| where response_code >= 500
| stats count by pool_member, response_code, virtual_server
| sort -count
description: Understanding which backends return 5xx errors helps isolate faulty application instances vs. systemic issues.
value: Understanding which backends return 5xx errors helps isolate faulty application instances vs. systemic issues.
grandmaExplanation: Understanding which backends return 5xx errors helps isolate faulty application instances vs ‚Äî so outages, slowdowns, and risky changes get flagged in time to act.
implementation: Enable HTTP response logging on the LB. Track 5xx rates per backend member. Alert when a single member's error rate exceeds the pool average by 3x. Auto-disable unhealthy members.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: F5 TA (`Splunk_TA_f5-bigip`), NGINX TA.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=f5:bigip:ltm:http`, `sourcetype=nginx:plus:api`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Enable HTTP response logging on the LB. Track 5xx rates per backend member. Alert when a single member's error rate exceeds the pool average by 3x. Auto-disable unhealthy members.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype="f5:bigip:ltm:http"
| where response_code >= 500
| stats count by pool_member, response_code, virtual_server
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Backend Server Error Code Distribution (F5 BIG-IP)** ‚Äî Understanding which backends return 5xx errors helps isolate faulty application instances vs. systemic issues.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=f5:bigip:ltm:http`, `sourcetype=nginx:plus:api`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): F5 TA (`Splunk_TA_f5-bigip`), NGINX TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: f5:bigip:ltm:http. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype="f5:bigip:ltm:http". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where response_code >= 500` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by pool_member, response_code, virtual_server** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (errors by backend), Table (member, error code, count), Timechart.
visualization: Bar chart (errors by backend), Table (member, error code, count), Timechart.
